extra.message: Flask secret_key is hardcoded in source code. An attacker who obtains the source can forge session cookies and bypass authentication.
User registration stores password in plaintext in the database without hashing. If the database is compromised, all user credentials are exposed.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The /dashboard route lacks any authentication check — any unauthenticated user can access it.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: HTML response is built by string concatenation and returned directly (no auto-escaping). Combined with reflected user input in other handlers, this pattern enables XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User-controlled input from request.form['expression'] is passed directly to eval(), allowing arbitrary Python code execution and remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The result of eval() on user-controlled input is reflected in the HTML response without escaping, causing reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The /evaluate endpoint — which performs eval on user input — has no authentication.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: User input from request.form['address'] is concatenated into a shell command ('nslookup ' + address) executed via popen/rp. An attacker can append shell metacharacters (e.g. '; rm -rf /') to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: The output of the nslookup command (which includes reflected user-controlled input) is written unescaped into the HTML response.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The /lookup endpoint which executes OS commands has no authentication.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: lxml etree.XMLParser is instantiated with no_network=False and dtd_validation=True, allowing parsing of external DTDs/entities. Passing user-supplied XML to etree.fromstring with this parser enables XML External Entity (XXE) attacks — file disclosure and SSRF.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: The parsed XML (derived from user input) is concatenated into the HTML response without escaping, causing reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from request.form['name'] is formatted into a template string that is then rendered via render_template_string(). This allows Server-Side Template Injection leading to arbitrary Python code execution via Jinja2.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The /sayhi endpoint vulnerable to SSTI has no authentication.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Flask debug mode is enabled (app.debug = True). This exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger, which allows remote code execution if an attacker can trigger an exception.
pickle module is imported but not used in current code. If re-introduced with user input it would enable insecure deserialization. (Informational — no active sink found.)
Login compares plaintext password directly from the database. No password hashing is performed. Combined with plaintext storage this results in credential exposure on database compromise.
